notes: 7 sovereigns (al, be, de, ee, li, mt, sm). Pulled 2026-06-11 on author intent: too many answers come from COA-internal black rather than the primary flag design — sm (San Marino) is a fresh example, plus Malta whose black-only-via-George-Cross drove the primaryCleanExempt: true escape hatch when this puzzle existed. Player who sees 'Europe + black' reasonably expects flags with visible black panels (be, de, ee) and gets confused by COA-only matches. Will unblock if the data convention shifts to tag emblem-internal colours distinctly, or if a future puzzle frames the COA dimension explicitly (e.g. 'Europe + coat-of-arms with black').

2 sovereigns (eg, ke). Pulled 2026-06-11 alongside Europe+black for the same reason — Egypt's black-via-Eagle-of-Saladin and Kenya's black-via-shield-decoration are both COA-internal, so 'Africa + black + COA' reads to the player as 'where does the black actually live?'. The puzzle's filter is internally consistent (it asks for both colour and COA) but the answer set is so small AND both flags lean on the COA for the black that the framing isn't clarified by the AND. Will unblock if a future Africa COA flag has visible black panels independently, OR if we author a clearer framing.

Romanian-style tricolour — Europe + blue/yellow/red + only 3 colours. Originally backlog #13; pulled 2026-06-11 when we tagged Andorra with `white` in additionalColors (the COA white). Andorra's full palette is now 4 colours so it fails colorCount:3, leaving Romania alone (1-flag, fails rule 9 size minimum). Will unblock if a second European flag adopts blue/yellow/red as exactly 3 primary colours; otherwise past-#100 recall candidate where 1-flag is allowed.

Italian-style tricolour worldwide — 11 flags (it, hu, mx, bg, ir, lb, dz, by, mg, mv, bi). Iconic palette puzzle (Mexico and Iran are the non-European entries that make this worldwide-interesting), but it's a filter-refinement of backlog #7 (continent:Europe + same colour pattern) so rule 6 blocks until past #100, OR until we decide to replace backlog #7 with this broader version.

Georgia alone (ge) — answer-incidental subset of backlog #46 (Asia red+white only-2 which includes ge). Refined rule 6 doesn't block this on filter grounds (different framing), but the puzzle is 1 flag and rule 9 forbids 1-flag puzzles before #50. Past-#100 candidate.

Iconic but {fi, gr} is a strict subset of live #1 (Europe·cross — both have crosses). Refined rule 6: filter is NOT a refinement of live #1's filter (different tokens), so technically allowed under refined rule. But park anyway — 2-flag puzzles with answer-incidental overlap of an iconic earlier puzzle still read as 'wait I already saw these' to the player.

19 default / 15 primary — NOT primary-clean (hr, sm, va, rs have yellow only in COA). Strict-superset of #8 (Europe RBY only-3 → ad,ro), #11 (Europe·star/moon → ba,hr,mk,si), and #23 (Europe BY only-2 → se,ua). Rule 5 (primary-clean) blocks until #101+; would need primaryCleanExempt to ever ship before then.

9 flags: am, bt, cy, ci, in, ie, ne, lk, zm. Primary-clean ✓. The !Oceania exclusion only drops Marshall Islands (mh, ns=6) — essentially 'orange flags worldwide minus the obscure one'. Strict-superset of #17 (OWG only-3 → cy,ci,ie,ne); refined rule allows since filter isn't a refinement. But color:orange is single-use (rule 14) and already used in backlog #18 — this puzzle can't ship. Past-#100 recall candidate.

9 non-African flags whose design features a triangle based on the hoist with apex pointing toward the fly: jo, ph, ps, tl, cz, bs, jm, gy, vu. (Jamaica is the structural odd one out — a saltire with four triangles converging on the centre rather than a single hoist triangle; included on author intent because all four triangles point centre-ward.) Explicitly NOT a motif — author has ruled out introducing `motif:hoist-triangle` because the geometric category is too specific to compound with other puzzles. The filter placeholder `geometry:hoist-triangle` doesn't parse today and there's no plan to make it. Will only ship via a future hand-curated-quiz mechanism (manual answer list) or a dedicated geometry DSL — until then, parked indefinitely. Pair with the Africa entry below.

9 African flags with the same hoist-triangle shape: km, dj, gq, er, mz, st, ss, sd, zw. Mean nm≈4.8 (three nm=6: Comoros, Djibouti, Equatorial Guinea) — categorically harder than the non-Africa pair and definitively a past-#100 puzzle. Same status as the non-Africa entry above: explicitly NOT a motif (author intent), the `geometry:hoist-triangle` placeholder is a wishlist marker, will only ship via a future hand-curated-quiz mechanism. Parked indefinitely until that exists. Author intent if it ever ships: non-Africa first as a stepping stone, this one rewards players who've internalised the African flag palette.

Blocked by data — only 1 sovereign carries violet at all (Dominica, dm), and only in additionalColors (the sisserou parrot's purple body). Primary-clean count is 0, so rule 5 blocks. Even ignoring rule 5, rule 9 forbids 1-flag puzzles before #50. Will unblock when ≥2 more violet-bearing flags exist OR a flag adopts violet as a primary colour. Until then, parked as a 'watch this list' candidate so we remember the option.

3 flags — bo, ec, gt (all South/Central American coat-of-arms with condor/quetzal + crossed weapons). Acknowledged rule violations, parked on author intent: (1) rule 14 — BOTH motif:bird and motif:weapon are single-use, already used in the live/backlog catalog (motif:weapon now used by the standalone 'all flags with weapons' puzzle at backlog #48 since 2026-06-13). Kept as a curiosity because the bird+weapon overlap is a real thematic category (Andean/Maya iconography). Will never ship as written unless the single-use rule is relaxed for parked-recall puzzles past #100.
